page: Amazon Web Services r5d.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity stands at 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance across several workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all fall within the middle 50% tier. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks show average performance, as do database operations and Redis SET operations, which reached 38.57 million operations per second.

Given the high memory density and the inclusion of 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, this instance is structured for memory-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space. The combination of 384.0 GB RAM and local NVMe storage makes it suitable for large databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing. While its single-core compute performance is limited, its resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that prioritize high memory capacity and local storage over raw single-threaded CPU speed.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 24 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the average tier, though single-core performance and single-threaded gzip compression fall into the bottom 25% tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 combined with local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications. This instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity and fast local storage rather than high single-threaded compute performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-capacity DDR4 RAM and local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database and data processing workloads.
